blocking_issues.title: Property test silently skips all non-string Sensitive fields — including the plan's own dict-typed Sensitive[T] annotation
blocking_issues.defect: The inner assertion loop guards on `isinstance(raw_value, str) and raw_value`. This silently exempts: (1) dict-typed Sensitive fields — exactly `Annotated[dict[str, Any], Sensitive(summarizer=...)]`, which is the annotation shape prescribed by the plan on `SetSourceArgumentsModel.options` (line 816) and repeated across the blob-tool wave; (2) non-string scalars (`int`, `bytes`, UUID after Pydantic coercion); (3) empty strings; (4) container-descent paths where value_provider returns `None` or `extracted is None` — both have silent `continue`s (lines 1578-1582). For dict-typed Sensitive fields, `node.value_provider(args)` returns the dict object; `isinstance(dict, str)` is False; the assertion is never reached. The property test claims to be the rev-2 BLOCKER_A closure for ALL promoted models via the `@pytest.mark.parametrize` loop over `MANIFEST`. With this gate, it exercises zero assertions on the most prevalent promoted-model Sensitive shape.
why_blocking: This is structurally identical to rev-3 B1: a load-bearing property test that returns green without exercising the assertion on the primary Sensitive annotation shape.
Cited explicitly as rev-2 BLOCKER_A closure in Done-When line 1716.
The four task-waves (4, 13, 14, 15) all use `Annotated[dict[str, Any], Sensitive(...)]` or `Annotated[str, Sensitive(...)]` on non-trivial fields; the property test must exercise both.

The fix is small: replace `isinstance(raw_value, str) and raw_value` with `raw_value is not None` guard plus `str(raw_value) not in serialized` assertion; OR assert `value_provider is not None` when the node carries a `_SensitiveMarker` (no silent skip for missing providers on marked nodes).
The per-tool Step 8 canary tests (Task 4) exercise one specific canary string on one tool. The property test must generalise; it currently doesn't for the dict case.
blocking_issues.resolution: Remove the `isinstance(raw_value, str) and raw_value` guard. For Sensitive nodes, `value_provider is None` must be a test failure, not a silent continue. For container nodes returning dict values, assert `json.dumps(raw_value, default=str)` does not appear in the serialized output (or that the summarizer output is what does appear). The `extracted is None` skip (line 1581) is acceptable only for genuinely-None Optional fields, but must be paired with `if node.value_provider is None: pytest.fail(...)` on marked nodes.

warnings.title: Rev-3 W7 (per-entry runtime smoke tests) not addressed
warnings.issue: No per-entry runtime call to `redact_tool_call_arguments(tool_name, sample_args, telemetry=...)` exists for the ~29–31 declarative manifest entries. Appendix C does not list W7 as closed. Task 16 sub-tasks add manifest entries and note `CAUTION: confirm with a unit test that the response shape always includes a content key` for `get_blob_content`, but there is no parameterised smoke test that actually calls through the runtime path per entry. The adequacy guard (Tasks 9–12) catches structural/consistency issues at import time but does not exercise the runtime walker on each entry's real argument shapes. The highest-risk shape remains `get_blob_content` with `sensitive_response_keys=('content',)`: a typo in the key string passes all adequacy assertions and is only caught at runtime when the response is processed.
warnings.fix: Add a `@pytest.mark.parametrize` test in `test_adequacy_guard.py` or a dedicated file that, for each declarative entry, constructs a minimal representative sample dict and calls `redact_tool_call_arguments(tool_name, sample, telemetry=NoopRedactionTelemetry())` asserting it returns a dict and does not raise. For entries with `sensitive_argument_keys`, assert the sensitive key value is absent from the output. This does not require full Hypothesis generation — a 5-line fixture per entry class suffices.

warnings.title: Rev-3 W8 partial: `Optional[Annotated[str, Sensitive()]]` scalar arm not tested
warnings.issue: Task 1's `_OptionalModel` uses `_FlatModel | None` — an optional BaseModel arm. `Optional[Annotated[str, Sensitive()]]` (optional scalar arm with the marker directly on the scalar) is a different annotation shape: `Optional[X]` where `X` itself has an `Annotated` wrapper. Plan appendix C does not list W8 as closed. If `walk_model_schema` unwraps `Optional` before checking `Annotated`, it may silently miss the `Sensitive` marker on the wrapped scalar.
warnings.fix: Add `class _OptionalSensitiveScalarModel(BaseModel): maybe: Optional[Annotated[str, Sensitive()]] = None` to `test_walk_model_schema.py`. Assert (a) a node with path `maybe` is yielded, (b) it carries `_SensitiveMarker`, (c) `value_provider` returns the string value when not None.

warnings.title: Rev-3 W8 partial: partial-walker-failure atomicity not tested
warnings.issue: No test asserts what happens when `walk_model_schema` raises mid-walk (e.g. a `_SensitiveMarker` on a field raises `ValueError` for duplicate markers, but the caller already emitted partial output). If `redact_tool_call_arguments` materialises the walker into a list before doing any substitution, partial output is impossible; if it zips with a generator, it may leave a partially-mutated dict. The plan does not specify atomicity.
warnings.fix: Document the atomicity contract explicitly in `redact_tool_call_arguments`'s docstring: either (a) the walker is materialised fully before mutation begins (safe), or (b) the mutation is idempotent and re-runnable. Add a test that a model with a bad-marker field (e.g. duplicate `Sensitive()`) causes the full call to raise without returning a partial result.

warnings.title: Rev-3 W8 partial: `redact_tool_call_response` with empty `known_response_keys` not tested
warnings.issue: Task 7 lists 'Response with all keys in known_response_keys and none in sensitive_response_keys: passthrough' but does not list the zero-key edge case: a declarative entry with `known_response_keys=()` receiving a response dict with any keys. The fail-closed sentinel path applies to unknown keys; a zero-length `known_response_keys` means ALL keys are unknown. The test should assert every key is sentinel-substituted and `telemetry.unknown_response_key_redacted` fires per key.
warnings.fix: Add one test case to `test_redact_tool_call_response.py`: declarative entry with `known_response_keys=()`, response `{"k": "v"}` — assert output is `{"k": "<redacted-unknown-response-key>"}` and telemetry fired once.

minor.title: Property test parametrize over MANIFEST at module import time
minor.issue: The `@pytest.mark.parametrize` decorates `test_no_sensitive_value_in_json_output` over `[name for name, entry in MANIFEST.items() if entry.argument_model is not None]` at module load time (line 1550-1553). When MANIFEST is populated incrementally across tasks 4-16, this parametrize list will be empty until Task 16 is complete. The test silently passes with 0 parametrized cases. This is correct behaviour during incremental development, but the Done-When criterion cites this test as closure; the executor should verify the parametrize list is non-empty when declaring the gate green.
minor.note: Not a plan defect per se, but an execution trap. Suggest adding an explicit assertion `assert list(MANIFEST.items())` or a collector count in the Task 19 final gate run.

minor.title: Telemetry sanity check in Task 19 is under-specified
minor.issue: Line 1650: 'with the production OtelRedactionTelemetry, run a representative compose-loop integration test and assert the named counters fire'. This is deferred to Task 19 without specifying which integration test or what OTel test infrastructure is used. If the project's OTel test harness uses a no-op meter by default (typical for unit tests), counter invocations may be no-ops and the assertion 'fire' cannot be verified. The plan should specify that a non-noop meter must be wired, or that the assertion is on the OtelRedactionTelemetry method calls via monkeypatch.
minor.note: The monkeypatch-based test in Task 3 (lines 595-622) is the correct shape for unit-testing counter invocations. The Task 19 phrasing implies an integration-layer test that may be difficult to wire correctly.

observations.title: Hypothesis `st.from_type(model)` strategy may generate `None` for Optional fields
observations.note: When `model` contains Optional fields, Hypothesis may generate instances where those fields are `None`. The test's `extracted is None` skip at line 1581 handles this correctly for non-Sensitive Optional fields, but if the Optional field itself is Sensitive and the generated value is `None`, the property test silently skips it. This is an acceptable limitation IF documented — `None` values cannot leak sensitive data — but should be called out in the test docstring to prevent future confusion.

B1_property_test_pathskip.evidence: The ad-hoc path.split('.') loop is replaced with node.value_provider (plan lines 1568-1580) — the structural fix from rev-3 is present. However, a new vacuous-pass mode is introduced at lines 1597-1602: `isinstance(raw_value, str) and raw_value` silently skips all dict-typed Sensitive fields (the plan's own primary annotation shape on SetSourceArgumentsModel.options) and all non-string scalars. The property test returns green without asserting redaction for the most common promoted-model Sensitive shape. Rev-3 B1 is structurally replaced, not closed: the specific path-traversal defect is fixed, but the property test still carries a silent-pass path for the load-bearing case. Treated as new BLOCKER by this review.

M1_iterator_completeness_oracle.evidence: Two independent coverage mechanisms are present: (1) test_walk_model_schema.py contains fixtures _FlatModel, _NestedModel, _ListModel, _DictModel, _OptionalModel, _UnionModel, _TupleModel with explicit path assertions across Annotated[scalar], Annotated[list], Annotated[dict], Optional[BaseModel] shapes; (2) Task 10 adds test_walker_emits_node_for_every_top_level_field asserting every model_fields key appears as a walk root for each type-driven MANIFEST entry, using model_fields as the oracle (different codepath from the iterator itself). Both mechanisms are spelled out as distinct tests in the plan. Appendix C confirms closure at Task 10. The 'separate codepath oracle' requirement is satisfied by the test_walk_model_schema.py fixture assertions (independent of the iterator's own path generation).

M2_manifest_membership_precondition.evidence: Task 17 lines 1364-1390 contain a verbatim test function `test_redact_tool_call_arguments_raises_for_unknown_tool` that calls `redact_tool_call_arguments('nonexistent_tool_name_for_call_order_pin', {}, telemetry=NoopRedactionTelemetry())` and asserts `AuditIntegrityError` with the tool name in the message. This is a 5-line mechanical test, not documentation. Listed as a Done-When criterion (line 1719) and in Appendix C. Test is in the correct file and asserts the expected error class. CLOSED.

W6_json_dumps_raise_risk.evidence: Task 4 lines 835-839 show `_summarize_set_source_options` uses `json.dumps(..., default=str)` explicitly. Task 4 Step 3a (lines 873-891) adds a dedicated test `test_summarize_set_source_options_accepts_coerced_datetime` that passes a `datetime` object in options and asserts the result is `str`. Plan docstring at line 833 cites spec §9 RSK-03 and explains the `default=str` rationale. CLOSED.

W7_per_entry_runtime_smoke.evidence: Appendix C does not list rev-3 W7. No per-entry runtime invocation of redact_tool_call_arguments or redact_tool_call_response for the ~29-31 declarative entries is present in the plan. Task 16 sub-tasks populate manifest entries and note a unit test for get_blob_content's content key, but do not prescribe parameterised runtime calls through the walker per entry. The adequacy guard (Tasks 9-12) exercises structural/consistency properties but not runtime walker dispatch per entry.

W8_optional_partial_empty_coverage.evidence: Optional[BaseModel arm] tested via _OptionalModel in test_walk_model_schema.py. NOT tested: (1) Optional[Annotated[str, Sensitive()]] scalar arm; (2) partial-walker-failure atomicity (no test or contract statement); (3) redact_tool_call_response with empty known_response_keys. Three distinct gaps remain. Appendix C does not list rev-3 W8. Plan appendix B W8 closure maps to 'Task 2 construction-time ValueError for both shapes set' which is a different W8 (rev-2 W8 = Sensitive[T] vs declarative precedence), confirming the rev-3 W8 is untracked in the closure appendix.

W4_tdd_discipline_from_task5.evidence: Preflight section (plan lines 33-35) contains the explicit meta-rule: 'TDD discipline applies to every task in this plan. Tasks 1-4 spell out the explicit step sequence: Step 1 — write the failing test; Step 2 — run, verify it fails; Step 3 — implement; Step 4 — run, verify it passes; Step 5 — gate; Step 6 — commit. Tasks 5-8 use compressed notation (Standard TDD task; test cases: [list]). The compression is readability shorthand, not an exemption — when executing those tasks, expand to the same six-step sequence.' This directly addresses rev-3 W4 (architecture A3). CLOSED.

notes: Rev-3 B1 is partially addressed: the node.value_provider substitution for ad-hoc path parsing is correct and present. However, the fix introduces a new silent-pass gate at the assertion level (isinstance(raw_value, str) and raw_value at plan lines 1597-1602) that exempts the plan's own primary Sensitive annotation shape — Annotated[dict[str, Any], Sensitive(summarizer=...)] on SetSourceArgumentsModel.options and the blob-tool wave. For those fields, value_provider returns a dict object; isinstance(dict, str) is False; the assertion body is never reached. The property test claims to be parametrized over all type-driven MANIFEST entries and to close BLOCKER_A universally, but it exercises zero assertions on the dict-Sensitive case. This is a new BLOCKER in this review (not carried forward — it is a regression introduced by the B1 fix). Three rev-3 warnings remain open: W7 (no per-entry runtime smoke, the highest-risk gap for get_blob_content key typos), W8a (Optional scalar arm not tested), W8b (atomicity unspecified), W8c (empty known_response_keys edge case). The telemetry surface (OtelRedactionTelemetry, OTel counter pattern, monkeypatch-based test) is correctly implemented per CLAUDE.md primacy and the established codebase pattern. TDD meta-rule is present and applies to all tasks. M1, M2, W4, W6 are genuinely closed.
